title: Preparing of genomic DNA from in vitro cultured cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5us05","text":"This protocol describes a standard procedure for preparing crude cell lysate which can be further analyzed by PCR.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dhift","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cga6p","text":"Protocol overview:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"38a44","text":"A. steps: Add 500 µl crude lysis buffer (Proteinase K added) to a fully confluent well of a 6 -well plate (~1.5 million cells)
Incubate at 37 °C 00:10:00
Collect the crude lysate into a 1.7 ml Eppendorf tube.
Incubate at 50 °C Overnight
Transfer 50 µl to a 200 µl microcentrifuge tube
Heat inactivate at 95 °C for 00:05:00 in a thermocycler.
Chill On ice
The crude cell lysate is ready for PCR
Crude lysis buffer A B 1 KCl 50 mM 2 MgCl2 2 mM 3 NP-40 0.45% 4 Tween-20 0.45% 5 Tris 10 mM 6 Proteinase K (add before use) 250 µg/ml
Wash cells with DPBS once
For each well in a 6-well plate, use 0.5 ml Trypsin
Incubate 37 °C 00:05:00
Inactivate Trypsin using 2 ml wash medium
Centrifuge at 200-300 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes
Remove supernatant
For a fully confluent well of a 6-well plate, mix the cell pellet with 500 µl crude lysis buffer (Proteinase K added). Reduce the amount of crude lysis buffer if the culture is not fully confluent.
Transfer the crude lysate into a 1.7ml Eppendorf tube.
Incubate at 50 °C Overnight
Transfer 50 µl to a 200 µl microcentrifuge tube
Heat inactivate at 95 °C 00:05:00 in a thermocycler
Chill On ice
The crude cell lysate is ready for PCR
Wash medium A B 1 DMEM/F12 470 ml 2 Newborn Calf Serum 25 ml 3 Penicillin & Streptomycin (100X) 5 ml Final volume; 500 ml
For small cell numbers, we usually use a direct cell to DNA extraction kit (Exact N Amp Blood PCR Kit [Sigma, # XNAT2-1KT]) according to the manufacturers’ instructions.
creator: Hanqin Li
